title: Writing Files
description: Create and write data to files.

slides.title: Saving Data 💾
slides.content:
# Writing to Files

Save data permanently:

```python
with open("output.txt", "w") as file:
    file.write("Hello, World!")
```

**Write modes:**
* `"w"` — Overwrite (creates new)
* `"a"` — Append to existing

slides.title: Writing Examples 💻
slides.codeSnippet:
# Simulating file writing
output = []

# Write mode (overwrites)
output.append("First line")
output.append("Second line")

print("Written to file:")
for line in output:
    print(line)

# Append mode would add to existing
output.append("Appended line")
print("\nAfter append:")
for line in output:
    print(line)

quizData.question: What does "w" mode do to an existing file?
quizData.explanation: "w" mode overwrites — existing content is lost!

slides.title: Writing Methods ✍️
slides.content:
# Different Ways to Write

| Method | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `write(str)` | Write a string |
| `writelines(list)` | Write list of strings |

**Note:** `write()` doesn't add newlines automatically!

slides.title: Writing Multiple Lines 💻
slides.codeSnippet:
# Writing multiple lines
lines = ["Line 1", "Line 2", "Line 3"]

print("Using write() with newlines:")
for line in lines:
    print(line + "\\n", end="")  # Add \n manually

print("\n\nUsing writelines():")
lines_with_newline = [line + "\n" for line in lines]
for line in lines_with_newline:
    print(line, end="")

quizData.question: Does write() add newlines automatically?
quizData.explanation: write() doesn't add newlines — you must include "\n" yourself!

slides.title: Append Mode 📝
slides.content:
# Adding to Existing Files

```python
# Append mode — doesn't overwrite!
with open("log.txt", "a") as file:
    file.write("New entry\n")
```

Perfect for log files!

slides.title: Be Careful! ⚠️
slides.content:
## Write Mode Danger

```python
# This ERASES everything in data.txt!
with open("data.txt", "w") as f:
    f.write("New content")
```

Double-check the mode before writing!

slides.title: Pro Tip 🚀
slides.content:
## Use print() to Files

```python
with open("output.txt", "w") as f:
    print("Line 1", file=f)
    print("Line 2", file=f)
```

print() adds newlines automatically!

quizData.question: Which mode adds to a file without erasing?
quizData.explanation: "a" is append mode — it adds to the end without erasing!
